name: TomTom Developer APIs
what_it_does: Comprehensive mapping and navigation API suite providing geocoding, routing, real-time traffic, places search, and turn-by-turn directions powered by TomTom's proprietary global map data.
use_cases: Route optimization for logistics agents calculating optimal delivery paths
Geocoding and reverse geocoding with TomTom's proprietary map accuracy
Real-time traffic data integration for ETA calculations in transportation agents
Isochrone calculation — finding all locations reachable within a given time or distance
Fleet management and tracking applications with map visualization
not_for: Consumer-facing map display without significant TomTom attribution compliance
Simple free geocoding at small scale (use OpenCage or Nominatim)
POI-heavy applications focused on social data (use Foursquare)
Applications already deeply invested in Google Maps ecosystem
best_when: You need routing, traffic, and mapping APIs as an alternative to Google Maps with competitive pricing, particularly for European automotive or logistics use cases where TomTom has strong data quality.
avoid_when: You need Google Maps' specific imagery, Street View, or ecosystem integrations, or your team is already invested in Google's mapping stack.

auth.notes: Single API key as query parameter across all TomTom API products. One key grants access to all enabled services under your account.

pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-as-you-go from $0.42-$5.00 per 1,000 transactions depending on service
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for development. Routing and matrix APIs are more expensive than geocoding. Enterprise pricing available for high-volume use. Competitive with Google Maps pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on service and plan; QPS limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Good documentation with examples. Error responses include error codes and descriptions. HTTP status codes follow standard conventions.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All routing and geocoding operations are read-only — fully idempotent given the same inputs.
known_agent_gotchas: API key in query parameter — exposed in URLs and server logs
Different API versions for different services — check endpoint versioning per service
Matrix routing (many-to-many) can be very expensive in transactions — plan cost carefully
Traffic data freshness varies by region — real-time accuracy better in EU than some other markets
Batch geocoding uses asynchronous job submission, not synchronous response
SDK is primarily for rendering maps (mobile/web) — server-side REST API has no official SDK

security_score_components.security_notes: Single API key. Location data subject to privacy regulations. SOC2 not prominent. Maps and routing data not sensitive but usage patterns may be.
